title: Cynanchum bahamense
taxonomy: Cynanchum bahamense belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the order Gentianales in the angiosperm division of plants. Within the Apocynaceae family, Cynanchum bahamense is classified as a genus of flowering plants. The genus Cynanchum is characterized by its twining or climbing habit and its distinctive flowers. Cynanchum bahamense specifically is native to the Bahamas.

This plant is valued for its traditional use in herbal medicine, particularly for its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and anxiety, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health.
